SAP Future of PMM Training
Chapter 6 Learning Guide
futureofpmm.com

Pricing, Packaging, Positioning

Key concepts and takeaways from Chapter 6

Learning Objectives

  • Understand how AI procurement analysts evaluate and compare pricing
  • Learn when pricing transparency becomes competitive advantage
  • Build packaging architecture that withstands automated analysis
  • Quantify value in ways AI can verify and cite

What AI Procurement Analysts Do

ActivityWhat AI DoesYour Response
TCO ModelingCalculates total cost including implementation, training, integrationsPublish your own TCO documentation
Competitive ComparisonPulls pricing from competitors who publish itBe the one who publishes, not the unknown
Term FlaggingIdentifies clauses that differ from industry standardAlign contracts with standard terms
Negotiation PrepSuggests specific asks based on discount patternsBuild defensible, value-based pricing

Pricing Transparency Strategies

  • Tiered clarity — Publish entry-level pricing, "starting at" for enterprise
  • Pricing calculators — Interactive tools for realistic estimates
  • Comparison pages — Show your pricing alongside competitors
  • TCO documentation — Help procurement's AI with your own models
Key Insight: When AI can't find your pricing, it recommends alternatives it can verify. Opacity is now a liability, not leverage.

Defensible Packaging Architecture

ElementBest Practice
Logical StructureClear use case per tier (SMB, Mid-market, Enterprise)
Clear Upgrade PathObvious reason to upgrade: more users, features, support
Defensible BundlingGroup features that genuinely belong together
Documented LimitsExplicit usage limits, not "reasonable use"

Value Claims That AI Cites

Weak ClaimAI-Verifiable Claim
"Significant cost savings""32% reduction in processing time (avg across 150 customers)"
"Improved efficiency""$2.4M annual savings for 1000-seat deployment"
"Better customer experience""15-point NPS improvement within 6 months"
"Industry-leading ROI""4.2x ROI in year one (Forrester TEI study)"

The Citation Test

For every value claim, ask: "Would an AI quote this in a procurement recommendation?"

  • If it's not specific enough to cite, it's not specific enough to convince
  • Third-party validation (Forrester, IDC) carries significant weight
  • Customer metrics with specific numbers get cited
  • Show the math behind ROI calculators

Key Takeaways

  1. Procurement AI models TCO, compares pricing, and prepares negotiations before talking to you
  2. Pricing transparency is becoming competitive advantage — AI recommends what it can verify
  3. Packaging architecture should be logical, clear, and machine-readable
  4. Value claims must be specific and verifiable to be cited by AI
  5. Third-party validated ROI studies carry significant weight in AI analysis
